steps.title: Fastest path: track the angle relationship first
steps.content: Track the given angle relationships first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are organized.

steps.title: Set up
steps.content: The angles of triangle $RST$ sum to $180^{\circ}$:$$42 + x + (5x - 6) = 180$$

steps.title: Solve
steps.content: Combine like terms: $36 + 6x = 180$, so $x = 24$. Angle $T = 5(24) - 6 = 114^{\circ}$. Since $\overline{LK} \parallel \overline{RT}$ and $ST$ is a transversal, angle $SKL$ and angle $T$ are corresponding angles, so angle $SKL = 114^{\circ}$.
